title: keeper-security-posture.v1 (dropped-design)
description: Dropped-design schema family. The 2026-04-26 design memo concluded that posture surface (BreachWatch / compliance / security-audit / weak-password / reused-password / expired-record reports) is intrinsically read-only and one-shot; declarative manifests cannot create a breach or assert a posture invariant idempotently. Surface ships instead as `dsk report <name>` runtime verbs per docs/V2_DECISIONS.md Q3. Scaffold retained (not deleted) so any manifest that pins this family fails validation with an explicit dropped-design status rather than a confusing 'no provider' error.

x-keeper-live-proof.notes: Replaced by `dsk report` verbs: compliance-report, security-audit-report, password-report, breachwatch-list, record-totp, enterprise-reports. See docs/V2_DECISIONS.md Q3 + the design memo (in daybook-sync repo: codex-prompts/_memos/2026-04-26_keeper-security-posture-v1-schema-design.md).
